title: ferray-linalg: cholesky missing upper= parameter
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's np.linalg.cholesky(a, upper=True) can return the upper triangular factor U where A = U^T U. ferray-linalg's cholesky() always returns the lower triangular L (hardcoded faer::Side::Lower). Add an upper parameter. Simple fix: transpose L if upper=True, or pass faer::Side::Upper.
